changesProposed.name: Caffeinated Capital

verified.name: Caffeinated Capital
verified.thesis: Caffeinated invests in seed and Series A rounds and throughout the life of technology companies. The firm supports founders building category-defining companies across all sectors.
verified.notes: Source [2] (Wikipedia) is about the chemical compound caffeine, not the VC firm, and is therefore not usable. Founded year is 2008 per source [1] (Raymond Tonsing bio: 'Raymond founded Caffeinated in 2008'), not 2018 as in database entry. AUM and flagship_fund cannot be verified from sources. Sector focus cannot be verified from explicit lists on the firm's site (sources describe specific portfolio companies across diverse sectors but no explicit sector focus list provided). Short name 'Caffeinated' appears on firm website but Wikipedia link is irrelevant. Three current senior partners identified on firm's team page.

changes.current: Concentrated lifecycle investing in orbital manufacturing, nuclear space, and defense. High-conviction bets on frontier defense and space companies.
changes.verified: Caffeinated invests in seed and Series A rounds and throughout the life of technology companies. The firm supports founders building category-defining companies across all sectors.

changesProposed.name: Construct Capital

verified.name: Construct Capital
verified.thesis: Construct invests in foundational industries where disruptive technologies have not been applied despite decades of neglect. The firm backs tech founders transforming manufacturing, logistics, defense, and energy sectors—the backbone of GDP.
verified.notes: Co-founded by Dayna Grayson (Managing Partner, former NEA investor) and Rachel Holt (Managing Partner, former Uber executive). AUM and flagship fund name not disclosed in sources. Sector focus extracted from portfolio categorization filters on firm website.

changes.current: Transforming foundational industries — manufacturing, logistics, defense infrastructure. Backing founders who modernize the physical backbone of the economy.
changes.verified: Construct invests in foundational industries where disruptive technologies have not been applied despite decades of neglect. The firm backs tech founders transforming manufacturing, logistics, defense, and energy sectors—the backbone of GDP.

changesProposed.name: DCVC (Data Collective)

verified.name: DCVC (Data Collective)
verified.thesis: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors, with focus on climate tech, computational biology, and automating dangerous industries. The firm invests at the intersection of AI and exascale computing applied to trillion-dollar problems in climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, defense and security.
verified.notes: Firm states '15 Years Active' on About page (source 1), suggesting founding around 2010-2011. Database entry lists founded_year as 2011 but this is not explicitly stated in sources. DCVC Bio established in 2018 as separate entity with four Managing Partners (source 1). Flagship fund name not mentioned in sources. Sector focus areas inferred from investment thesis and portfolio companies but not explicitly listed as formal sector focus on firm website.

changes.current: Exclusively deep tech. Investing at the intersection of computational approaches and massive datasets applied to trillion-dollar industries: agriculture, energy, manufacturing, transportation, and life sciences.
changes.verified: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors, with focus on climate tech, computational biology, and automating dangerous industries. The firm invests at the intersection of AI and exascale computing applied to trillion-dollar problems in climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, defense and security.

changesProposed.name: Eclipse Ventures

verified.name: Eclipse Ventures
verified.thesis: Eclipse invests in entrepreneurs building companies in the physical economy that determine resilience, competitiveness, and security. The firm partners with innovators transforming the physical economy, bringing operational experience in building factories, shipping hardware, and scaling supply chains.
verified.notes: Source [4] is Wikipedia article about astronomical eclipses, not the VC firm - irrelevant. Founded year (2015), AUM ($4B+), flagship fund (Eclipse Fund IV), and HQ (Palo Alto, CA) from database entry could not be verified in provided sources. Sector focus could not be extracted from explicit sector lists in sources. Partner 'Lior Susan' from database not found in current team listing [2].

changes.current: Industrial transformation. Investing in companies that apply technology to modernize the physical world — from defense manufacturing to construction robotics to supply chain intelligence.
changes.verified: Eclipse invests in entrepreneurs building companies in the physical economy that determine resilience, competitiveness, and security. The firm partners with innovators transforming the physical economy, bringing operational experience in building factories, shipping hardware, and scaling supply chains.
